_comment: The expected install footprint of a PINNED Spec Kit. Its purpose is to make upstream drift
obvious on the next release: spec-kit ships multiple releases a day, and a footprint that
changes silently is how a collision reaches a user project.
This is checked by bin/speckit-footprint.sh, which is OWNERSHIP-aware. The naive rule --
'after specify init, nothing but speckit-* may change' -- is wrong twice: speckit-* files are
SUPPOSED to appear (that is the integration working, not a collision), and the Claude
integration's documented footprint includes an agent-context file (CLAUDE.md). So we compare
OWNERS, not names: same path + same owner = expected; same path + different owner = collision.
_owned_note: Every path matching expected_owned_patterns must ALSO be claimed by Spec Kit's own manifest.
A speckit-*-looking file that Spec Kit does not claim is the collision case: same name,
_conditional_note: Reported and shown to a human, never auto-rejected. The Claude integration is multi_install_safe
and writes an agent-context file. Rejecting this would reject documented, correct behavior --
but accepting it silently would hide a real change to the file that governs the project.
_forbidden_note: Jaimitos state. If Spec Kit ever writes any of these, it has become a second orchestrator and
the integration is a REJECT -- no discussion, no mitigation.
_context_note: Spec Kit's Claude skills ship with `disable-model-invocation: false`, so every one of their
descriptions sits in the model's window EVERY TURN, in the user's project, forever. Jaimitos
caps its own model-invoked descriptions at 6000 B (test-skills.sh check 6) -- but that check
iterates only the toolkit's skills/ directory and CANNOT SEE these. There is no supported way
to exclude a Spec Kit core command. This is REJECT criterion R1, and the footprint check
MEASURES it rather than estimating it.

measure_always_loaded.note: Only skills WITHOUT `disable-model-invocation: true` cost always-loaded context.
